title: Requirement
description: A single mapped requirement inside a scope.

text.description: Paraphrased requirement text. Do NOT redistribute copyrighted standards verbatim.

text_url.description: Direct link to the canonical text of this requirement.

pryv_effort_saved.description: How much of the requirement Pryv carries (high = Pryv saves most implementer effort; low = small substrate; bulk on implementer). Required for every coverage tier except out-of-scope (which is zero by definition). See docs/facilitation-typology.md.

facilitation_mode.description: What kind of help Pryv provides for this facilitated requirement. Only meaningful when coverage='facilitated'. See docs/facilitation-typology.md.

overview.description: High-level position (2-3 sentences). Audience: auditor / compliance officer. Skimmable; states pryv's contribution without jargon.

detail.description: Functional explanation (medium length). Audience: compliance officer + technical lead. Why + how at the feature level; multi-aspect breakdowns, gotchas, configuration trade-offs.

technical.description: Implementation-level specifics. Audience: engineer. Code paths, primitive citations beyond pryv_primitives[], exact API methods, version constraints.

draft.description: True when authored but not yet user-reviewed.

items.description: Test code as used in open-pryv.io test descriptions (e.g., 'EVTC').
Relative path into dev-site/src/ or compliance-matrix/docs/ (may include #anchor).
Primitive identifier matching a heading in docs/pryv-primitives.md (e.g., 'access', 'permissions', 'audit', 'CMC').
Sample app demonstrating the claim end-to-end (relative path under compliance-matrix/samples/<slug>/, or external URL). Use when tests + functional specs don't cover the user-experience evidence.

planned.description: Planned bug fixes or backlog features whose shipping would change this row's claim. WAB renders one chip per entry alongside the coverage/effort badges; index/scope pages surface a filter for rows with planned items. Multiple entries allowed (a row may depend on both a queued bug fix and a backlog feature).

kind.description: bug = known defect already queued for fix (the row's current claim is partially incorrect); feature = backlog feature whose shipping would tighten / extend the claim; enhancement = smaller refinement (docs, ergonomics).

summary.description: One-line description of what's queued (rendered in the WAB chip tooltip).

proposal.description: Relative path to the matrix-side proposal markdown under proposals/ (e.g., 'proposals/audit-on-user-delete.md').

backlog.description: Slug of the upstream backlog file (e.g., 'AUDIT-ON-USER-DELETE'). Omit if the planned work has no upstream backlog yet.

impact.description: How much this row's claim shifts when shipped. high = coverage tier likely changes; medium = effort/facilitation upgrade; low = wording / evidence refinement.

tracking_url.description: Optional external tracker (GitHub issue, project board) — supplements the in-tree backlog file.

applies_to_versions.description: semver-range constraint of open-pryv.io versions this row describes (e.g., '>=2.0.0').

items.description: Cross-scope reference (e.g., 'gdpr.Art.5') indicating this requirement inherits evidence from another scope's row.
